title: Timing and Spacing of Immunobiologics | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:

Pneumococcal polysaccharide vaccine (PPSV23)
Rabies vaccine
Recombinant zoster vaccine (RZV)
Respiratory syncytial virus vaccine (RSV)
Serogroup B meningococcal (MenB) vaccines (MenB-FHbp, MenB-4C)
Tetanus-toxoid, diphtheria-toxoid, or pertussis-containing vaccines (DTaP, Tdap, DT, Td, DTaP-HepB-IPV, DTaP-IPV/Hib, DTaP-IPV, DTaP-IPV-Hib-HepB)† 
Non-replicating § 
COVID-19 vaccine (Janssen)
Jynneos smallpox/monkeypox vaccine 
(*) Oral adenovirus vaccine is used primarily in the military for prevention of adenovirus infection. It should not be confused with Janssen COVID-19 vaccine which is used for the prevention of SARS-CoV-2 infection.
(†) The tetanus-toxoid components of these vaccines are toxoids, not vaccines.
(§) These vaccines do not replicate and therefore behave like non-live vaccines.
